name: leafcast
title: Leafcast
description: Control your Nanoleaf panels with Raycast! Toggle power, set the current scene, and more.
author: syropian

commands.name: pair-device
commands.title: Pair Device
commands.subtitle: Leafcast
commands.description: Pairs your Nanoleaf device with Raycast

commands.name: control-lights
commands.title: Control Lights
commands.subtitle: Leafcast
commands.description: Control various aspects of your Nanoleaf light panels

commands.name: identify
commands.title: Identify Device
commands.subtitle: Leafcast
commands.description: Briefly flash the paired Nanoleaf panels to confirm which device is paired

commands.name: toggle-power
commands.title: Toggle Power
commands.subtitle: Leafcast
commands.description: Toggle the Nanoleaf panels on or off

commands.name: next-effect
commands.title: Next Effect
commands.subtitle: Leafcast
commands.description: Cycle to the next effect in your Nanoleaf device's list

commands.name: previous-effect
commands.title: Previous Effect
commands.subtitle: Leafcast
commands.description: Cycle to the previous effect in your Nanoleaf device's list

tools.name: set-power
tools.title: Set Power
tools.description: Turn the Nanoleaf panels on or off.

tools.name: set-brightness
tools.title: Set Brightness
tools.description: Set the Nanoleaf panel brightness to a value between 0 and 100.

tools.name: set-color
tools.title: Set Color
tools.description: Set the Nanoleaf panels to a specific solid color (named, hex, or rgb/hsl).

tools.name: set-effect
tools.title: Set Effect
tools.description: Apply a named effect/scene to the Nanoleaf panels.

tools.name: list-effects
tools.title: List Effects
tools.description: List the effects/scenes available on the Nanoleaf device.

tools.name: get-state
tools.title: Get State
tools.description: Read the current power, brightness, and active effect of the Nanoleaf panels.

tools.name: identify
tools.title: Identify Device
tools.description: Briefly flash the Nanoleaf panels to identify which device is paired.

ai.instructions: These tools control the user's Nanoleaf light panels. When the user mentions 'lights', 'panels', or 'leafcast', assume they mean the Nanoleaf panels. For color requests, prefer set-color and pass the most natural representation of the requested color (named CSS color, hex, or rgb). For scene/pattern/mood requests, use set-effect — if the requested effect name isn't an obvious match, call list-effects first to disambiguate. Map vague brightness language ('dim', 'bright', 'cozy') to a numeric value before calling set-brightness.

preferences.name: deviceAddress
preferences.description: The IP address of your Nanoleaf device
preferences.title: Device IP

preferences.name: maintainBrightnessOnColorChange
preferences.description: Maintains brightness when setting the panels to a solid color. Useful for having a consistent brightness level on camera.
preferences.label: Maintain brightness when changing colors
